---
title: API Reference
description: Complete API reference for @justwant/env.
icon: BookOpen
---

## Imports

```ts
// Node.js / Bun (full — includes file loading and watch)
import { createEnv, defineEnv, EnvironmentError } from "@justwant/env";

// Edge (no fs, no watch)
import { createEnv, defineEnv, EnvironmentError } from "@justwant/env/edge";
```

## createEnv

```ts
function createEnv<T extends EnvSchema, G extends GroupSchema | undefined>(
  options: CreateEnvOptions<T> & (G extends undefined ? {} : { groups: G })
): CreateEnvResult<T, G>
```

### CreateEnvOptions

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `vars` | `EnvSchema` | `{}` | Record of variable schemas |
| `groups` | `GroupSchema` | — | Namespaced variable groups |
| `groupSeparator` | `string` | `"_"` | Separator between group prefix and key in env |
| `include` | `EnvInclude[]` | `[]` | Reusable env blocks from `defineEnv` |
| `sources.files` | `string[]` | auto | `.env` files to load |
| `sources.processEnv` | `boolean \| Record<string,string>` | `true` | Use `process.env` or an object |
| `sources.inline` | `Record<string,string>` | — | Hard-coded values (highest priority) |
| `cwd` | `string` | `process.cwd()` | Directory for resolving env files |
| `expand` | `boolean` | `true` | Expand `${VAR}` references |
| `clientPrefix` | `string \| string[]` | — | Strip prefix when reading env keys |
| `modes` | `Record<string, (string \| number \| symbol)[]>` | — | Required keys per mode |
| `mode` | `string` | `NODE_ENV` | Active mode |
| `redact` | `string[] \| RegExp` | — | Keys to hide in `toJSON()` |
| `validation.onError` | `"throw" \| "warn" \| "silent" \| false` | `"throw"` | Error handling |
| `validation.skip` | `boolean` | `false` | Skip validation entirely |
| `validation.reporter` | `(issues) => void` | — | Custom issue reporter |
| `watch` | `boolean` | `false` | Watch files for changes (Node/Bun only) |
| `onReload` | `(changed: string[]) => void` | — | Called when files change |

### CreateEnvResult

The result extends the validated variables directly, plus proxy methods:

```ts
type CreateEnvResult<T, G> = InferEnv<T> & (G extends GroupSchema ? InferGroupEnv<G> : {}) & EnvProxy;
```

## defineEnv

```ts
function defineEnv<T extends EnvSchema>(options: DefineEnvOptions<T>): EnvInclude<T>
```

Creates a reusable env block. Pass it to `createEnv`'s `include` option.

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `vars` | `EnvSchema` | Variable schemas |
| `clientPrefix` | `string \| string[]` | Prefix for client-safe variables |

## EnvProxy

Methods available on every `createEnv` result:

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `get` | `(key, fallback?) => unknown` | Dynamic typed access; dot notation for groups (`"db.host"`) |
| `has` | `(key) => boolean` | Check if a key is present |
| `raw` | `(key) => string \| undefined` | Raw string value before validation |
| `toJSON` | `() => Record<string, unknown>` | Plain object; sensitive keys redacted if configured |

## EnvSchema

```ts
type EnvSchema = Record<string, StandardSchemaV1<unknown, unknown>>;
```

A record mapping variable names to `@standard-schema/spec` validators. Any Zod, Valibot, or ArkType schema implements this interface.

## GroupSchema

```ts
type GroupSchema = Record<string, EnvSchema>;
```

## InferEnv

```ts
type InferEnv<T extends EnvSchema> = {
  [K in keyof T]: T[K] extends StandardSchemaV1<unknown, infer O> ? O : never;
};
```

Infer the output types from an `EnvSchema`.

## InferGroupEnv

```ts
type InferGroupEnv<G extends GroupSchema> = {
  [Group in keyof G]: { [K in keyof G[Group]]: ... };
};
```

## EnvironmentError

Thrown when `validation.onError === "throw"` and there are invalid or missing variables.

```ts
class EnvironmentError extends Error {
  readonly issues: { key: string; message: string }[];
}
```

```ts
import { EnvironmentError } from "@justwant/env";

try {
  createEnv({ vars: { PORT: z.string() } });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof EnvironmentError) {
    for (const issue of err.issues) {
      console.error(issue.key, issue.message);
    }
  }
}
```
